title: Stroke Rehabilitation
content: Stroke rehabilitation helps people relearn skills affected by stroke, including movement, speech, strength, daily living skills, thinking, and communication. Rehabilitation planning depends on the person's needs and may include physical therapy, occupational therapy, speech-language therapy, cognitive therapy, counseling, and support from a care team.

title: Home Health Services
content: Medicare home health services can include part-time or intermittent skilled nursing care, physical therapy, occupational therapy, speech-language pathology, medical social services, and certain aide services when skilled care is also being provided. A provider must order care and a Medicare-certified home health agency must provide it.

title: Non-Emergency Medical Transportation
content: Medicaid non-emergency medical transportation is a benefit intended to help Medicaid beneficiaries get to covered medical services when they have no other transportation. Caregivers should confirm scheduling rules, covered appointment types, and broker or plan contact information before relying on a ride.

title: National Family Caregiver Support Program
content: The National Family Caregiver Support Program supports family and informal caregivers through information, help accessing services, counseling, training, support groups, respite, and supplemental services where available. Local aging and disability networks often connect families to these supports.

title: Montgomery County Senior Services Network
content: Montgomery County Senior Services Network describes local support services for older adults, including programs connected to home safety, case management, referrals, transportation, and services that may help older adults remain at home safely. The county directs residents to the Area Agency on Aging for service information.

title: Montgomery County Senior Transportation Services
content: Montgomery County describes senior transportation support connected to medical visits and community activities through local providers. Families should ask local providers or the Area Agency on Aging which locations participate, what rides are available, and whether advance scheduling is required.
